VitalEdge

Fitness & Wellness Centre · Group Assignment 2 · Deliverable 3

Thando Kamo Belinda Sonke Munashe
↓scroll to explore
Thando

Company Narrative

VitalEdge is a South African fitness and wellness company built around a unique sponsor-funded membership model.

The Model

Members do not pay monthly fees. Health insurers and corporate employers sponsor member access and are only billed when members meet their verified monthly fitness milestones. If a member fails, the sponsor does not pay. The member pays out-of-pocket or faces a tier downgrade.

The Platform

Each branch offers gym floor access, group fitness classes, and personal training. Members enrol at a primary branch and may visit others within their access tier. Every branch runs a supplement and gear shop with stock tracked per branch and automatically replenished when levels drop below threshold.

Loyalty & Billing

Members earn loyalty points when milestones are verified as passed, redeemable in the shop while access is active. All transactions flow through VitalEdge's centralised billing system with separate invoices generated per sponsor per cycle.

Kamo

Business Rules

Eight rules directly shaped the database design. The six most critical ones are below.

1

Primary Branch Enrollment

A member enrolls at exactly one primary branch. Cross-branch visits are capped at four per month at no extra cost. Beyond that, the walk-in day rate applies. Tracked via is_primary_branch and BRANCH_VISIT.

2

Milestone-Based Sponsor Funding

Sponsor funding is conditional on meeting the monthly milestone threshold, calculated strictly from verified, non-flagged session logs. Failure means the sponsor does not pay. Enforced via MILESTONE_REVIEW.is_pass.

3

Dual Sponsor Model

At most one health insurer and one corporate employer per member simultaneously. Combined contributions cannot exceed the full standard rate. Separate invoice per sponsor per cycle. Enforced via CHECK (contribution_pct BETWEEN 0.01 AND 1.00).

4

Trainer Capacity

A trainer may have a maximum of twelve active client assignments at any time. A session booking is only valid if the assigned trainer is employed at the branch where the session takes place.

5

Session Logging Compliance

Trainers must log session details within 24 hours. Unlogged sessions are flagged and excluded from milestone calculations. Trainers exceeding five unlogged sessions per month face compliance reviews and reduced assignments.

7

Loyalty Points

Points are only awarded when a milestone review is passed. Redemption requires active access and a passing most-recent review. Enforced via CHK_Loyalty_Exclusive_Source.

Kamo

D2 Modifications

Six feedback points received. Some were already correct in our original design. Valid feedback was implemented.

ChangeWhat happenedStatus
amount_contributed_pctRenamed and changed data type from DECIMAL(10,2) to DECIMAL(5,4) to correctly represent percentages (0.5000 = 50%)✓ Implemented
INVOICE_TYPE lookupIntroduced a normalised INVOICE_TYPE entity with FK relationship. Invoice types no longer stored as raw strings.✓ Implemented
Relationship lineINVOICE to INVOICE_SPONSOR_CONTRIBUTION updated from dashed to solid, correctly showing a strong identifying relationship✓ Implemented
Business rules refinedRules 2, 5, 6 tightened. Verification specifics and trainer compliance consequences added. Loyalty expiry rule added.✓ Implemented
MEMBER uniquenessFeedback said email/phone uniqueness was missing. The U symbol was already present in our original D2 ERD. No change needed.Already correct
LOYALTY_TRANSACTION dual referenceFeedback said dual reference was not enforced. The transaction_type discriminator was already in our original ERD. No change needed.Already correct

Milestone & Loyalty

Training

Inventory & Shop

  • SUPPLIER + BRANCH_STOCK
  • REPLENISHMENT_ORDER
  • SHOP_TRANSACTION / TRANSACTION_LINE
Sonke

Database Implementation

Built in Microsoft SQL Server with named constraints, correct data types, and enforced business rules.

35 Tables · 3NF · SQL Server

Every FK named · Every constraint enforced

ConstraintTableCodePurpose
Composite UNIQUEMEMBERUNIQUE (email, phone)Prevents duplicate registrations
Composite UNIQUEMEMBER_ENROLLMENTUNIQUE (member_id, branch_id)One enrollment per member per branch
CHECKMEMBER_SPONSORcontribution_pct BETWEEN 0.01 AND 1.00Enforces Business Rule 3
CHECKSPONSORsponsor_type IN ('health_insurer','corporate_employer')Only two valid sponsor types
TABLE-LEVEL CHECKLOYALTY_TRANSACTIONCHK_Loyalty_Exclusive_SourceEnforces Business Rule 7
Named FKsAll tablesCONSTRAINT FK_Staff_Branch...Readable errors, easier maintenance

Query 1 — Subquery (NOT IN)
Which products have never been purchased? Identifying dead stock using a subquery.

      

        Min price filter (R)
        
        Show dead stock with unit_price ≥ this value
      

      
SELECT product_id, product_name, unit_price
FROM PRODUCT
WHERE product_id NOT IN (
    SELECT DISTINCT product_id FROM TRANSACTION_LINE
)
AND unit_price >= 0
ORDER BY unit_price DESC;

      
      

        
✓ 3 rows returned

        

          
product_idproduct_nameunit_price

          
5Premium Yoga MatR 600.00

          
2Pre-Workout EnergyR 300.00

          
4Training GlovesR 250.00

        

      

    


    
    

      
Query 2 — Multiple INNER JOINs
Active members at a selected branch as their primary gym and their current tier.

      

        Branch
        
        Status
        
      

      
SELECT m.first_name + ' ' + m.last_name AS 'Member Name',
       m.email, b.branch_name, at.tier_name AS 'Current Tier'
FROM MEMBER m
INNER JOIN MEMBER_ENROLLMENT me ON m.member_id = me.member_id
INNER JOIN BRANCH b ON me.branch_id = b.branch_id
INNER JOIN ACCESS_STATUS ast ON m.member_id = ast.member_id
INNER JOIN ACCESS_TIER at ON ast.access_tier_id = at.access_tier_id
WHERE b.branch_name = 'VitalEdge Rosebank'
  AND me.is_primary_branch = 1
  AND ast.status_code = 'active'
ORDER BY m.last_name ASC;

      
      

        
✓ 2 rows returned

        

          
Member NameEmailbranch_nameCurrent Tier

          
Ruan Botharuan.b@email.comVitalEdge RosebankGold

          
Heinrich Mullerheinrich.m@email.comVitalEdge RosebankSilver

        

      

    


    
    

      
Query 3 — CASE statement with GROUP BY
Trainer capacity monitoring enforcing Business Rule 4.

      

        Capacity Alert threshold
        
        Optimal min
        
        Max is always 12 per Business Rule 4
      

      
SELECT s.first_name + ' ' + s.last_name AS 'Trainer Name',
       COUNT(DISTINCT sl.member_id) AS 'Active Clients',
       CASE
           WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT sl.member_id) > 10 THEN 'Capacity Alert'
           WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT sl.member_id) BETWEEN 6 AND 10 THEN 'Optimal'
           ELSE 'Available'
       END AS 'Capacity Status'
FROM STAFF s
JOIN PERSONAL_TRAINER pt ON s.staff_id = pt.staff_id
JOIN SESSION_LOG sl ON pt.trainer_id = sl.trainer_id
GROUP BY s.first_name, s.last_name
ORDER BY 2 DESC;

      
      

        
✓ 4 rows returned

        

          
Trainer NameActive ClientsCapacity Status

          
Pieter van Zyl2Available

          
Sarah Jenkins1Available

          
Naledi Khumalo1Available

          
Priya Naidoo1Available

        

      

    


    
    

      
Query 4 — Stored Procedure with IF/ELSE
Checks if a sponsor will pay for a given member and cycle. Directly enforces Business Rule 2.

      

        Member ID
        
        Billing Cycle
        
      

      
CREATE PROCEDURE CheckMilestoneBilling
    @TargetMemberID INT,
    @TargetCycleID INT
AS BEGIN
    DECLARE @PassStatus BIT;
    SELECT @PassStatus = is_pass
    FROM MILESTONE_REVIEW
    WHERE member_id = @TargetMemberID
      AND billing_cycle_id = @TargetCycleID;

    IF @PassStatus = 1
        PRINT 'SUCCESS: Member passed. Proceed with billing the Sponsor.';
    ELSE IF @PassStatus = 0
        PRINT 'WARNING: Member failed. Sponsor will NOT pay.';
    ELSE
        PRINT 'ERROR: No milestone review found.';
END;

-- Testing: change member ID and cycle above then click Run
EXEC CheckMilestoneBilling
    @TargetMemberID = 6,
    @TargetCycleID = 2;

      
      

        
⚠ Messages

        
WARNING: Member failed. Sponsor will NOT pay. Bill member out-of-pocket or downgrade tier.
Completion time: 2026-05-29T09:09:12.5329652+02:00

      

    


    
    

      
Query 5 — CREATE VIEW
Branch revenue summary. Filter by branch or show all.

      

        Filter branch
        
        Sort by
        
      

      
CREATE VIEW branch_revenue_summary AS
SELECT
    b.branch_name,
    SUM(i.amount_due) AS 'Total Billed',
    ISNULL(SUM(p.amount_paid), 0) AS 'Total Collected',
    SUM(i.amount_due) - ISNULL(SUM(p.amount_paid), 0) AS 'Outstanding Balance'
FROM BRANCH b
JOIN MEMBER_ENROLLMENT me ON b.branch_id = me.branch_id
JOIN INVOICE i ON me.member_id = i.member_id
LEFT JOIN PAYMENT p ON i.invoice_id = p.invoice_id
GROUP BY b.branch_name;

-- Query the view with optional filter and sort
SELECT * FROM branch_revenue_summary
ORDER BY Outstanding Balance DESC;

      
      

        
✓ 3 rows returned

        

          
branch_nameTotal BilledTotal CollectedOutstanding Balance

          
VitalEdge Rosebank2797.001998.00799.00

          
VitalEdge Menlyn649.000.00649.00

          
VitalEdge Sandton1449.001199.00250.00

        

      

    


    
    

      
Query 6 — EXCEPT Set Operator
Active members who have not visited this cycle. At-risk of cancelling. EXCEPT chosen over NOT IN for NULL safety.

      

        Billing Cycle
        
        Shows active members with zero visits in selected cycle
      

      
SELECT m.member_id, m.first_name + ' ' + m.last_name AS Member_Name,
       m.email, 'At Risk of Cancelling' AS Member_Status
FROM MEMBER m
INNER JOIN ACCESS_STATUS ast ON m.member_id = ast.member_id
WHERE ast.status_code = 'active'
EXCEPT
SELECT m.member_id, m.first_name + ' ' + m.last_name, m.email,
       'At Risk of Cancelling'
FROM MEMBER m
INNER JOIN BRANCH_VISIT bv ON m.member_id = bv.member_id
WHERE bv.billing_cycle_id = 1;

      
      

        
✓ 6 rows returned

        

          
member_idMember_NameemailMember_Status

          
4David Smithdavid.s@email.comAt Risk of Cancelling

          
5Amina Patelamina.p@email.comAt Risk of Cancelling

          
6Ruan Botharuan.b@email.comAt Risk of Cancelling

          
7Tshepo Motsepetshepo.m@email.comAt Risk of Cancelling

          
8Chloe Govenderchloe.g@email.comAt Risk of Cancelling

          
9Bongani Sibiyabongani.s@email.comAt Risk of Cancelling
